item.title: Live Updates: Knicks celebrate championship win with parade in NYC
item.description: The New York Knicks are being honored with a ticker-tape parade at 10 a.m. Thursday through the Canyon of Heroes in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: Arthur, 2026's first tropical storm, weakens but still poses flooding threat
item.description: Arthur, the first named tropical storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, weakened to a post-tropical cyclone after making landfall.

item.title: Knicks legends and Alicia Keys among those who will be at victory parade
item.description: On Thursday, New York City is celebrating the Knicks' NBA championship with a victory parade. Knicks legends like Walt "Clyde" Frazier and Patrick Ewing will be in attendance and Alicia Keys will perform to close it out.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic ramps up in wake of U.S.-Iran deal signing
item.description: More tankers are transiting the Strait of Hormuz after 111 days of gridlock, but Iran and the U.S. still have much to discuss.

item.title: Breaking down Luigi Mangione's new defense strategy for NY state trial
item.description: CBS News legal contributor Caroline Polisi discusses the new defense strategy for Luigi Mangione's New York state murder trial and what it could mean for the case. Mangione's lawyers told a judge he was suffering from extreme emotional disturbance when he allegedly killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

item.title: Victims' families speak at Gilgo Beach killer sentencing: "Felt like this day would never come"
item.description: Rex Heuermann, the man known as the Gilgo Beach serial killer, was sentenced Wednesday to life in prison without parole. Families of the victims spoke out at his sentencing. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Ukraine drone strike hits major Russian oil refinery near Moscow
item.description: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says after second major strike on Russian oil refinery that unless Putin stops his war, "Moscow will burn."

item.title: Read the 14 points of the agreement between Iran and the U.S.
item.description: The agreement, as read by senior U.S. officials, allows Iran to immediately begin exporting oil and petroleum products.

item.title: 1,200-year-old Robin Hood oak tree in Sherwood Forest has died, group says
item.description: A massive ancient oak tree linked to the legend of Robin Hood may have been loved to death.

item.title: Extended interview: Singer-songwriter Myles Smith
item.description: British singer-songwriter Myles Smith talks to Anthony Mason about his much-anticipated debut album, "My Mess, My Heart, My Life," growing up in London, his rapid rise to stardom and more.

item.title: Chicago man faces arson, hate crime charges after cross burning in Grant Park
item.description: The flames from the cross burning in the middle of Grant Park were visible to anyone driving or walking down Columbus Drive.

item.title: Inside an Irish town raising kids without smartphones
item.description: When parents and educators in Greystones, Ireland saw children dealing with increasing anxiety, they acted – and took phones out of the equation.

item.title: Arrests of immigrant parents create mental health crisis for children
item.description: An estimated hundreds of thousands of children, many of them U.S. citizens, have been separated from a parent in the Trump administration's immigration crackdown.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Tornadoes tear through Illinois, Iowa as severe weather roils Midwest
item.description: Confirmed tornadoes were reported in Illinois and Iowa Wednesday as severe weather brought the risk of thunderstorms and prompted a flurry of tornado warnings for a large swath of the Midwest​.

item.title: Trump formally signs U.S.-Iran deal as questions linger about nuclear program
item.description: President Trump warned he could order new strikes if Iran's leaders "don't behave." The U.S. and Iran signed the memo of understanding remotely, a White House official said.

item.title: This law firm has deep ties to Trump. Now a partner is his pick for a top job.
item.description: Jamie McDonald, a partner at the firm Sullivan & Cromwell, is President Trump's choice to helm one of the nation's most prestigious federal prosecutors' offices.

item.title: South Australia premier says youth social media ban is working, 6 months later
item.description: Six months after Australia implemented the world's first-ever social media ban for kids under 16, South Australia Premier Peter Malinauskas joins "The Daily Report" to discuss the changes he's seen among teens.

item.title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
item.description: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.

item.title: New York Knicks championship parade expected to draw more than a million people
item.description: A record crowd is expected to turn out in Lower Manhattan on Thursday for the New York Knicks' long-awaited championship parade. The NYPD will be there too, treating it as a high-security event. Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: How Trump's memo of understanding with Iran compares to the Obama nuclear deal
item.description: The Obama-era Iran deal was packed with technical details and specific requirements limiting, but not shutting down, Iran's nuclear program. President Trump's new agreement is more of a framework, and it's not final yet.

item.title: Why Trump told DNI pick Jay Clayton to skip Senate hearing
item.description: The Senate delayed its confirmation hearing Wednesday for director of national intelligence nominee Jay Clayton after Republican Sen. Tom Cotton said President Trump directed Clayton not to appear. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.

item.title: Veteran speaks out on wife's ICE detention, possible deportation: "It makes me sick to my stomach"
item.description: ICE recently detained the wife of a retired Army staff sergeant and could deport her, despite her husband's nearly two decades of service to the U.S. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the story.

item.title: Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George leads in Democratic primary for Washington, D.C., mayor
item.description: Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George holds nearly a 20-point lead in the Democratic primary for Washington, D.C., mayor, with 68% of the vote in. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion spoke with Lewis George about her vision for the nation's capital.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue psychiatric defense in state murder trial
item.description: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue that he was suffering from an extreme emotional disturbance when he allegedly killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

item.title: 6/17: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Terms of U.S.-Iran deal revealed by U.S. officials; Fed keeps interest rates steady in first decision under Chair Kevin Warsh.

item.title: Oklahoma pastor drops out of House race after texting scandal
item.description: Jackson Lahmeyer had been backed by President Trump, although the president dropped his endorsement shortly before Lahmeyer dropped out.

item.title: The Obama Presidential Center and Library opens in Chicago
item.description: After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026.

item.title: Is Russia burning through air defense missiles at an "unsustainable rate"?
item.description: Ukraine intelligence indicates the country's stepped up drone strikes are forcing Russia to burn through interceptor missiles, possibly faster than it can build new ones.

item.title: U.K. announces plan to ban social media for children under 16
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer hopes to have a ban on kids using social media that is "designed to be addictive" enacted by early next year.
